stem: A food manufacturing study measures the weight (grams) for a sample of 15 cereal boxes. The sample mean is 321.9 g with a sample standard deviation of 76.6 g. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 299.0 g and standard deviation of 56.9 g. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 321.9, 95% CI = [279.5, 364.3]. Without outliers: mean = 299.0, 95% CI = [267.4, 330.6]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [267.4, 364.3]. Point estimate: 310.4 g.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [267.4, 364.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
